title: Competitive Intelligence - ACE Inhibitor Market India
content: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE: ACE Inhibitors in India (2024-25). Market leader: Generic Enalapril (40% market share by volume) due to lowest price point (Rs 1.5/tablet). Lisinopril generic competition: 15+ manufacturers including Cipla, Sun Pharma, Lupin, Dr Reddy's. Price range: Rs 2-8 per tablet depending on brand. Our differentiation vs competitors: (1) Once-daily dosing (Enalapril is twice-daily). (2) Patient assistance program — 30% co-pay reduction for BPL card holders. (3) Stronger diabetic nephropathy data (EUCLID trial). (4) Physician education program with CME credits. Key threat: ARBs (Losartan, Telmisartan) gaining market share due to no-cough advantage. ARB market growing at 12% CAGR vs ACE inhibitor 4% CAGR. Counter-strategy: Position Lisinopril on outcomes data + renal protection + cost advantage vs ARBs.

title: Competitive Intelligence - Diabetes First-Line Market India
content: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE: Metformin Market in India (2024-25). Metformin is the undisputed first-line therapy — 90%+ of T2DM patients start on Metformin. Over 100 manufacturers in India. Price: Rs 0.50-3 per 500mg tablet. Key competition is NOT other molecules but other Metformin brands. Major competitors: Glycomet (USV), Glucophage (Merck), Obimet (Zydus). Our differentiation: (1) Extended-release formulation with better GI tolerability. (2) Combination packs with DPP-4 inhibitors. (3) Patient compliance program with pill organizer kits. (4) Doctor education on CV outcomes (UKPDS 34 data). Emerging threats: SGLT2 inhibitors (Dapagliflozin) showing CV and renal benefits. Some endocrinologists starting SGLT2 as first-line in high-CV-risk patients. Counter: Metformin's 60-year safety record, lowest cost, ICMR guideline first-line status.

title: Competitive Intelligence - CCB Market India
content: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE: Calcium Channel Blockers in India (2024-25). Amlodipine dominates the CCB market with 75%+ share. Major competitors: Amlong (Micro Labs), Amlokind (Mankind), Stamlo (Dr Reddy's). Price: Rs 1.5-6 per 5mg tablet. Highly commoditized. Our differentiation: (1) Brand trust and physician loyalty program. (2) ALLHAT + ASCOT data positioning for evidence-based prescribing. (3) Combination with ACE inhibitor — reduces edema while adding renal benefit. Key threat: Cilnidipine gaining share — marketed as 'no edema CCB' (N+L type). Cilnidipine growing at 25% CAGR. Heavily promoted by Ajanta, Glenmark. Counter: Cilnidipine has limited long-term outcomes data vs Amlodipine's ALLHAT evidence.
